reasons: Angela can attach to Hayabusa during his dive and give him a safer burst window when he commits onto a backline target.
Hayabusa benefits from extra protection because his main value often happens after he has already entered enemy threat range.

proof.summary: Hayabusa creates the assassination window, while Angela adds protection during the dive.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before or during Hayabusa's real commit.
Hayabusa keeps a usable shadow or escape route after the burst attempt.
failureCases: Hayabusa dives into layered crowd control.
Hayabusa loses his shadow exit path before the protection can matter.

reasons: Mathilda can help Hayabusa approach or disengage with extra mobility support.
Hayabusa's dive pattern becomes safer when he has another movement route besides his own shadows.

proof.summary: Mathilda gives Hayabusa extra mobility and shield support, making his dive and reset pattern more reliable.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda positions near Hayabusa's entry route or escape route.
Hayabusa commits after Mathilda can support the path.
failureCases: Hayabusa commits too deep before Mathilda can provide a usable route.
The enemy controls Hayabusa before he can use the mobility support.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag a priority target, giving Hayabusa a clean single-target follow-up window.
Hayabusa's burst is more reliable when the target is controlled and separated from safe positioning.

proof.summary: Kaja starts the pickoff with suppression, and Hayabusa follows with burst before the target can reposition.
worksBestWhen: Kaja catches a damage dealer or mobile target before Hayabusa commits.
Hayabusa is close enough to follow the drag immediately.
failureCases: Kaja pulls the target into too many enemy bodies, reducing Hayabusa's clean isolation window.
Hayabusa's damage is split across nearby enemies.

reasons: Franco can pull or suppress one target, giving Hayabusa a direct burst target without forcing Hayabusa to start the engage alone.
The pair works well around bushes, river, and objective entrances where one caught target can become a fast kill.

proof.summary: Franco creates the catch, while Hayabusa follows with burst and cleanup.
worksBestWhen: Franco catches a target away from minions or frontliners.
Hayabusa waits for the confirmed hook or suppression before entering.
failureCases: Franco catches a durable frontline target.
Hayabusa's burst is split across nearby enemies.

reasons: Floryn can support Hayabusa even when he dives far from the team because her healing can reach allies anywhere on the map.
Hayabusa benefits from global backup when he commits to side-lane pickoffs, jungle invades, or deep backline executions.

proof.summary: Hayabusa can dive or rotate far from the team, while Floryn's global healing gives him emergency sustain during or after the commit.
worksBestWhen: Floryn saves Bloom for Hayabusa's real kill attempt or escape window.
Hayabusa has enough exit path to use the healing value.
failureCases: Hayabusa is suppressed, chain-controlled, or bursted before the heal matters.
Floryn uses Bloom too early for minor poke damage.
